title: Daily Briefing — Wednesday, June 10
tldr: Dev play of the day: give your terminal AI real code intelligence. GitHub Copilot CLI now drives language servers (LSP) — so the agent gets IDE-grade semantics (go-to-definition into dependencies, find-all-references, project-wide rename, type resolution) instead of grepping and guessing. Setup is itself an AI Skill: install the LSP Setup skill, say "set up LSP for Python", run /lsp. Fewer hallucinated symbols, reliable renames, far less context burned.

Paid — read Meta's Opportunity Score before you touch a setting. Meta rolled its 0–100 Opportunity Score to all advertisers: a structural-health grade (creative variety, signal quality, audience breadth, conversion-event accuracy) that lets you triage an account in 10 seconds. And it halved the Advantage+ learning-phase bar to ~25 conversions/week (from 50) — so smaller accounts can finally run the unified flow. It grades setup, not results — keep judging on cost-per-purchase.

Social — write LinkedIn so AI search engines cite you. Semrush analyzed 89K LinkedIn URLs cited in AI answers (ChatGPT Search, Google AI Mode, Perplexity): LinkedIn is the #2 most-cited domain (behind Reddit), and the winners are original 500–2,000-word articles, posted consistently — not viral feed posts. LinkedIn is now a GEO surface; ~95% of cited content is original.

News — the AI-trade repricing rolls on. AI-chip stocks kept sliding Jun 10 (MU −4%+, AMD −3.5%, NVDA/INTC −2%+) on higher-for-longer rate fears + geopolitics — the market is grading AI spend, not ambition. Yet private capital is rotating into physical AI: Neura Robotics raised up to $1.4B (Tether-led, with Nvidia/Amazon/Qualcomm) at a reported ~$7B valuation.

News — Claude Fable 5 is public, with a retention catch. Anthropic's most capable model (~80.3% SWE-Bench) is now GA in GitHub Copilot, Bedrock and Databricks — but Fable-5-class models run under a new 30-day data-retention policy and existing Zero-Data-Retention agreements don't apply. Microsoft restricted it for employees over exactly this. Check which model your tools call before sending client-confidential data.

Sharpen your edge: today's throughline is verify the substrate before you trust the system — give the agent the real code graph (LSP) so it stops guessing; read the setup score before optimizing; confirm a model's data terms before you feed it secrets. Capability is necessary; provenance and ground-truth are what make it safe to ship.

sections.h: Web dev — run it today: give your terminal AI real code intelligence (Copilot CLI + LSP)

blocks.sub: GitHub Copilot CLI can now drive language servers — ask it to 'set up LSP for <language>' and it stops guessing
blocks.p: What changed (Jun 2026): GitHub shipped a Language Server Protocol (LSP) integration for Copilot CLI, its terminal coding agent. Until now an agent in the terminal navigated your code by grep and heuristics — reading whole files into context and guessing at types. With a language server wired in, the agent gets the same precise, semantic understanding your IDE has: go-to-definition (including into third-party libraries), find-all-references, project-wide rename, type resolution across dependencies, and hover docs — returned as compact structured results instead of dumping entire files into the conversation. Setup is itself an AI Skill: GitHub ships an "LSP Setup skill" (a reusable markdown runbook with YAML frontmatter) that installs and configures the server for you, covering 14 languages. Why it matters for our builds: agentic/terminal coding is where a lot of AI dev work now happens (CI fixes, refactors, headless agents), and the #1 failure mode is the agent hallucinating a symbol or editing the wrong call site because it never really "saw" the code graph. LSP turns the agent from a fuzzy text-searcher into something that actually resolves your types — fewer wrong edits, reliable renames, and far less context burned reading files it didn't need.
blocks.doIt: (1) Install the LSP Setup skill: extract it to ~/.copilot/skills/ and restart Copilot CLI. (2) Ask in plain English — set up LSP for Java or enable code intelligence for Python — and the skill installs the right server (e.g. npm i -g typescript typescript-language-server, or brew install jdtls for Java) and writes config to ~/.copilot/lsp-config.json (user) or lsp.json (repo). (3) Run /lsp to confirm the server is up. (4) Test it: ask the agent to "find all references to X" or "rename X across the project" and watch it resolve into your dependencies. (5) Commit the repo-level lsp.json so every teammate's agent shares the same code intelligence.
blocks.note: It's the agentic-IDE version of a play we already keep — setup-as-a-Skill (a markdown runbook the AI loads on demand). Watch for Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Cursor to standardize an equivalent LSP hook (logged as a prediction). Card: card-webdev-copilot-cli-lsp.

sections.h: Paid — run it today: read Meta's Opportunity Score before you touch a single setting

blocks.sub: Meta's 0–100 Opportunity Score is now in Ads Manager for everyone — and Advantage+ now qualifies at ~25 conversions/week, not 50
blocks.p: What changed: Meta rolled out the Opportunity Score to all advertisers — a 0–100 grade on each campaign in Ads Manager for how closely your setup follows Meta's best practices across four dimensions: creative variety, signal quality (Pixel / Conversions API), audience breadth, and conversion-event accuracy. Crucially it's a structural health check, not a performance verdict — a 95 means well-built, not profitable. Separately, Meta halved the learning-phase bar for Advantage+ Shopping/Sales: campaigns now qualify at ~25 conversions/week, down from 50 — which makes the unified Advantage+ flow viable for far smaller budgets (this resolves the "thresholds reportedly easing" note we'd carried as directional since Jun 7). Why it matters for us: it's a 10-second triage before a call — open the account, sort by Opportunity Score, and the low scorers tell you exactly what's structurally broken (fragmented audiences, thin creative, a misconfigured Pixel) before you waste time eyeballing ROAS. And the lower conversion bar means clients you previously told "you don't have the volume for Advantage+" can now run it.
blocks.doIt: (1) In Ads Manager, add the Opportunity Score column (or open a campaign's overview) and triage — anything under ~60 has real structural gaps; aim for 80+. (2) Raise it the documented way: turn on Advantage+ audience, placements and creative; upload a deep, varied creative pool; fix tracking with the Conversions API. (3) Treat the score as a setup checklist, NOT a KPI — keep judging delivery on cost-per-purchase, and keep one small manual ad set as your control to prove the AI's lift. (4) For smaller accounts, re-evaluate Advantage+ Sales now that ~25 conv/week clears the learning phase.
blocks.note: Opportunity Score grades best-practice alignment, not results — a high score with weak creative still loses money. Treat the exact 25/week figure as directional; Meta's Help Center lags real behavior, so confirm on the account. Card updated: card-paid-meta-advantage-plus.

sections.h: Social — run it today: write LinkedIn so AI search engines cite you

blocks.sub: Semrush analyzed 89K LinkedIn URLs cited by AI — LinkedIn is the #2 most-cited domain, and original 500–2,000-word articles win
blocks.p: What changed: a new Semrush study (325,000 prompts across ChatGPT Search, Google AI Mode and Perplexity, Jan–Feb 2026) found LinkedIn is the #2 most-cited domain in AI answers, behind only Reddit — roughly 11% of AI responses cite a LinkedIn URL (ChatGPT Search 14.3%, Google AI Mode 13.5%, Perplexity 5.3%). It reverse-engineers what gets cited: articles dominate (50–66% of cited LinkedIn content; feed posts 15–28%), with 500–2,000-word articles cited most; educational/advice content is 54–64% of citations; cited posts had only moderate engagement (15–25 reactions — relevance beats virality); ~75% of cited authors post frequently (5+ posts / 4 weeks); and ~95% of cited content is original (reshares are just 5%). Why it matters for us: buyers increasingly ask ChatGPT/Perplexity "who's good at X" — and the answer is being pulled from LinkedIn. That makes LinkedIn a Generative Engine Optimization (GEO) surface, not a vanity feed: a client's or partner's LinkedIn presence is now a discovery channel into AI answers, and it's winnable with craft, not ad spend.
blocks.doIt: (1) List the 3–5 questions a buyer would ask an AI in your space ("best [service] for [use case]", "how to [outcome]"). (2) For each, publish a LinkedIn article (not just a feed post) of 500–2,000 words that answers it directly — lead with a clean one-sentence answer, then expand with original POV + your own data. (3) Post consistently — cited authors averaged 5+ posts in 4 weeks; you don't need virality, moderate relevant engagement is enough. (4) Reuse assets: paste a blog post or talk transcript into Claude and ask for a LinkedIn article in your voice (see the repurpose-with-Claude card), then add first-party numbers. (5) Spot-check: ask ChatGPT/Perplexity your target question and see whether you (or a competitor) get named — iterate.
blocks.note: This is the social half of the AEO/GEO story we've tracked in growth — same principle (structure original, authoritative answers so a model can lift and attribute them), now on LinkedIn. Card: card-social-linkedin-ai-citations (threaded with the AEO content-formats card).

sections.h: News — the landscape: the AI-trade repricing rolls on, robots get $1.4B, and Fable 5 ships with a retention catch
sections.intro: Trust-critical domain — figures cross-checked against a 2nd source and marked directional where fast-moving.

blocks.sub: AI-chip stocks keep sliding — the 'grade the spend' regime, not a one-day dip
blocks.p: What happened (Jun 10): the AI-chip complex kept falling — premarket, MU −4%+, AMD −3.5%, NVDA and INTC −2%+ — extending last week's drop (the Nasdaq shed ~4% on a hot jobs report that revived "higher-for-longer" rate fears), with added pressure from US–Iran geopolitical headlines. Jensen Huang reiterated that AI-chip demand stays strong "for years." Why it matters to us: this is the same thread we flagged Jun 8 — the market is now grading AI spend, not AI ambition. Our open call (the sell-off is a dip, not a regime change; due ~Jul 20) is still live and still under pressure. The practical read for client work: lead every AI pitch with cost-per-outcome and a spend ceiling (see the AI-gateway spend-limit card) — buyers are in scrutiny mode.
blocks.note: Directional — intraday percentages move hour to hour and age fast (premarket Jun 10 via TipRanks; last week's Nasdaq −4% via Yahoo Finance). The durable fact is the direction + the why (rates + caution), corroborated across sources.

blocks.sub: Capital is rotating into embodied AI: Neura Robotics raises up to $1.4B
blocks.p: What happened: German humanoid-robotics maker Neura Robotics announced a Series C of up to $1.4B at a reported ~$7B valuation — said to be the largest round ever for a full-stack robotics company — led by Tether with Nvidia, Amazon, Qualcomm, Bosch, Schaeffler and the European Investment Bank participating (the full amount is milestone-contingent). Robotics startups have raised $55.8B in 2026 YTD (Dealroom), nearly double last year's record. Why it matters: even as public AI equities sell off, private capital is pouring into physical AI — the money is rotating from "LLMs" toward embodied/robotics and the power + compute to run it. A signal for where the next wave of tooling and client demand shows up, not a trade.
blocks.note: Confirmed across CNBC, Tech.eu and Neura's own release; the ~$7B valuation is from a single anonymous source — treat as reported. "Up to $1.4B" is milestone-contingent, not all upfront.

blocks.sub: Claude Fable 5 is public — but it carries 30-day data retention, and Microsoft restricted it internally
blocks.p: What happened: Anthropic released Claude Fable 5 publicly (Jun 9) — its most capable model, ~80.3% on SWE-Bench — now generally available in GitHub Copilot, AWS Bedrock and Databricks. The catch for teams handling sensitive data: Fable-5-class models run under a new 30-day data-retention policy (classifiers retain prompts/outputs for 30 days, flagged items up to two years), and existing Zero-Data-Retention (ZDR) agreements do NOT apply to Fable 5 traffic. Microsoft has restricted Fable 5 for its own employees — it's pulled from the internal Copilot model picker while legal reviews the change, while other Claude models that still run under ZDR remain available. Why it matters to us: this is the "two gates" rule in the wild — a model can pass the capability/cost gate (Fable 5 is excellent) and fail the data-provenance gate. If you or your tools route client-confidential material through Fable 5 expecting zero retention, that assumption is now wrong.
blocks.doIt: Before pointing any client-data workflow at Fable 5: (1) check which model your tool / Copilot / IDE is actually calling (Fable 5 may be a new default in some pickers); (2) for confidential client material, prefer a Claude model still covered by ZDR (or another ZDR/enterprise path) until terms are confirmed; (3) read Anthropic's Mythos-class retention page and confirm your enterprise agreement's coverage in writing.
blocks.note: Reported via The Verge/Reuters (Microsoft restriction) + Cybernews + Anthropic's own Help Center (retention terms) — corroborated. Card: card-ai-tooling-fable5-retention.

sources: GitHub Blog — Give Copilot CLI real code intelligence with language servers · GitHub Docs — Using LSP servers with Copilot CLI · Social Media Today — Meta launches Opportunity Score to all advertisers · SocialBee — 2026 Meta & Facebook updates (Advantage+ 25-conversion threshold) · Semrush — We analyzed 89K LinkedIn URLs cited in AI search · PPC Land — How LinkedIn content earns AI-search citations (Semrush) · TipRanks — Why AI chip stocks NVDA/MU/AMD/INTC are falling (Jun 10, 2026) · CNBC — Neura Robotics raises up to $1.4B from Nvidia, Amazon and others · Tech.eu — Neura Robotics secures up to $1.4B Series C · The Verge — Microsoft restricts Claude Fable 5 internally over data retention · TechCrunch — Anthropic releases Claude Fable 5 publicly · Anthropic — Data retention practices for Mythos-class models · Cybernews — Fable 5 data retention: no exceptions
